CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN APACHE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell over the White Mountains of east-central Arizona, with 12 inches of snow accumulating at a local ski area.
Gradient wind gust to 60 kts reported at 12:35 MST at the St. John's Airport.  No wind damage reported.
A strong winter storm dropped 10 inches of snow at the North Rim of Grand Canyon National Park.  Wind speeds ranged from 20 to 35 mph and wind chills were down to 20 below zero.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Wind Damage in Flagstaff, AZ.  Non-convective winds knocked down trees, large branches, and power lines.  No estimated or measured wind speeds at damage location.  No damage estimate.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN APACHE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Wind Damage in Springerville, Arizona.  A brief gradient wind gust ripped the roof off one mobile home and knock several other mobiles homes off their foundations.  Structural movement broke several water lines and caused extensive damage to trailer skirting.  Many anchors and tie-down cables used to secure mobile homes were snapped.  There was no estimated or recorded wind speed.  There were no other reported high wind incidents in the vicinity.  The event lasted less than one minute.
Non-convective wind gusts measured to 50 kts/58mph at the St. Johns airport.  No damage reported
Blowing dust reduced visibility to near zero which caused 25 vehicles to pile up on Interstate 10 about 10 miles south of Phoenix.

A dry cold front moving through the area brought widespread strong winds to southeast Arizona. Low visibilities in blowing dust were common with some areas near zero visibility. A roof was blown off a 200 foot long agriculuture shed near Bowie.
Non-convective winds gusted in excess of 50 kts/58 mph measured at the St. John's airport.  Peak gust of 59 kts/68 mph occurred at 16:40.
Thunderstorm winds caused damage to 10 mobile homes at the Silveridge RV Resort in East Mesa.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm produced 6 to 15 inches of snow across the Mogollon Rim of Central Arizona.  Snowfall amounts included 11 inches in Flagstaff and 15 inches in Parks, 35 miles west of Flagstaff.  Winds were recorded at 15 to 30 mph producing wind chills as low as 20 below zero.  Hazardous driving conditions produced by the storm caused over 100 traffic accidents within a 30 mile radius of Flagstaff.  Only minor injuries were reported.

A strong winter storm dumped heavy snow across Arizona's central high country. Some snowfall totals include 10 inches in Payson, 12 inches in Pine, 18 inches in Strawberry, and 6 inches in Prescott. There were reports of vehicles skidding off icy roadways.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN NAVAJO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm dumped heavy snow across Arizona's central high country. Some snowfall totals include 10 inches in Payson, 12 inches in Pine, 18 inches in Strawberry, and 6 inches in Prescott. There were reports of vehicles skidding off icy roadways.
Large dust devil destroyed an aluminum shed.
Strong gradient winds associated with a vigorous storm system produced wind damage in Poston. Three power poles snapped and several trees blew over.
Thunderstorm winds snapped several power poles.
Thunderstorm wind gusts experienced at Lake Montezuma.
A thunderstorm with heavy rains caused flash flooding in the Lake Mead National Recreation Area.  Mud and rocks blocked the Willow Beach access road for about two hours.
Funnel cloud reported by the public and law enforcement.
Tornado touched down on the outskirts of Kayenta.  A few small shed were blown down and there was minor roof damage to out-buildings.  No damage estimate available.
Hail covered the ground to 5 inches deep.
Funnel cloud reported by the public and law enforcement.
Wind gusts to 71 mph were recorded at Bullhead City Airport from a nearby thunderstorm.
Tornado briefly touched down. Reported by the public and law enforcement officers.  No damage reported.
Funnel cloud reported by public and law enforcement.
Wildfire outbreak described in Coconino County entry.
Wildfire outbreak described in Coconino County entry.
Waterspout on Woods Canyon Lake damaged several boats and boat docks.
Thunderstorm wind gust to 70 mph/61 kts at St. Johns Airport.
Thunderstorm wind gust to 40 kts snapped a power pole.  The pole fell on a mobile home causing a fire.  The mobile home was extensively damaged by the pole and the fire.
Strong high pressure produced strong gusty winds over southeastArizona.

A hiker in the Huachuca Mountains had a lightning strike hit extremely close to him. He was thrown off his feet and could not move for several seconds. The thunder caused his ears to ring for many days afterward.

A strong thunderstorm downbusrt in Thatcher and Safford created the following damage. Crop damage, trees down and broken, power lines down, roof damage, one mobile home blown off its blocks, one mobile home flipped over, small signs and awnings blown away, billboard blown into new cars, flooding of some apartments, and structure fire caused by lightning.  Rainfall was estimated at 11/2 to 2 inches with hail nearly covering the ground.
Half of a roof was blown off a house in Tucson near River and Campbell streets. No thunderstorms in area so most likely cause was a very strong dust devil.
Two people struck by lightning 3 miles south of Tucson International Airport.  Minor injuires, no deaths.
Storm blew down several large trees at 114th Avenue and Bell Road.Wind gust estimated from damage.
Straight-line winds damaged a floating wet dock and sank several boats in the Roosevelt Lake area. Wind gust estimated from damage.
A few power lines were blown down near the intersection of Price Rd. and Dobson Rd. Wind gust estimated from damage.
Large tree limbs were blown down by thunderstorm winds. Wind gust estimated from damage.
Power lines were blown down at 52nd St. and Cactus Rd. Wind gust estimated from damage.

A wildfire, called the Side Fire, was human-caused and wind-driven. Over 200 recidents were evacuated when the fire moved within 200 feet of 2 subdivisions in east Flagstaff.  No homes were damaged, but 320 acres were burned.

A wildfire, called the Horseshoe Fire, began at the sight of an old slashpile burn and was driven by gradient winds, estimated at 60 mph.  In 7 days the Horseshoe Fire destroyed 8650 acres of public and private land.  No damage estimate was available.
The Slate Fire was human-caused, but wind driven.  The fire began on April 30 and was controlled on May 1.  No structures were damaged, but 275 acres of timber were destroyed.
Gradient wind gusts were measured to 65 mph at the St. John's Airport.
Flash flood of Poland Creek in Crown King and several roads in Crown King.  No damage or injuries.

Wildfire outbreak. Two days of numerous dry thunderstorms, June 20 and 21, started over 120 lightning-caused fires. By the end of June, over 82,000 acres of private and public land was burned. Most were controlled by June 23, but several burned through the end of June until July 6 when the first significant rain occurred. The most significant wildfires were:BRIDGER KNOLLS/JUMP Fire: started at 1700, 06/20, controlled 07/06. Acres burned by end of June: 53,500, on the Northern Rim of the Grand Canyon.HOCHDERFFER FIRE: started 06/20, controlled 07/03. Total acres burned: 16,400. Twelve miles NW of Flagstaff.POT FIRE: started 06/20, controlled 06/26. Total acres burned: 7000. Ten miles NE of Sedona.COTTONWOOD FIRE: started 06/20, controlled  06/25. One mile south of Pinedale. Total acres burned: 1586.
Wildfire outbreak described in Coconino County entry.
Wildfire outbreak described in Coconino County entry.
Storm toppled numerous power poles. Wind gust estimated from damage.

A roof was ripped off a home, a shed was overturned, and part of a fence was uprooted. Several utility poles along Pinal Road near Rodeo Road were downed by the high winds. Wind gust estimated from damage.
Several downed power poles near 43rd Ave. and Interstate 10. Wind gust estimated from damage.

An Aircoupe aircraft was damaged beyond repair when strong winds flipped the plane over. Minimal damage was sustained by two other planes when one blew into the other. Out of a total of 116 hangars at the Chandler Airport, 24 were damaged. Two hangars had door sections torn loose. One hangar door was blown 200 feet.
Dozens of 60-foot power poles were knocked down and numerous reports of damage to mobile homes and rooftops.

Heavy rain created flash flooding in Kingman causing street closures and some road damage.  A few cars were abandoned but no injuries were reported.  Also, the combination of rain and hail produced slippery roads resulting in numerous automobile accidents.
Numerous reports of golfball-size hail were received with hail piling up to 4 inches deep in some locations.
A skywarn spotter reported 3/4 inch diameter hail covering the ground.

A tornado observed near Kingman, AZ touched down in a field and caused significant damage to a barn and tool shed.  Doors were torn from their hinges, windows were broken and part of the roof was blown off.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN APACHE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High southwest winds were reported near St. Johns.  St. Johns airport reported a peak gust of 58 knots (67 MPH) at 1900 MST.  The strong winds caused the drivers of several vehicles around the Springerville area to lose control and were blown off the highway.No significant damage was reported.

Very strong north to northeast winds caused significant damage and combined with cold temperatures to produce extremely cold wind chills above 9000 feet in the San Francisco Peaks area north of Flagstaff. The highest wind gust of 90 kt (105 mph) was recorded by the Arizona Snowbowl ski area at 0300 MST at an elevation of 10,800 feet. Winds blew down approximately 80 trees in the Arizona Snowbowl. One fallen tree caused several thousand dollars damage to a ski lift. Communication lines and power were down also for several hours on the morning of the 18th.
Flash flood of a creek which runs through the town of Humboldt. One individual tried to cross the creek, became trapped in his vehicle and needed to be rescued by fire and law enforcement personnel.
A storm spotter reported a funnel cloud 2 miles northeast of 40th Street and Union Hills.
Two trees were blown down and a large wooden board was blown onto a police car.
Several trees were blown down.
Several APS power poles were blown down at Cottonwood and Casa Grande Ave. Also a report of a roof blown off a house.
A carport was ripped off by the strong thunderstorm winds.
1.75 inch hail reported near Three Points, just west of Tucson.
1.75 inch hail reported at Tucson Estates. Wind damage also reported by law enforcement.
Normally dry washes quickly filled to near bankfull due to very heavy thunderstorm rains. Three teenagers were rescued from one of these washes in Ehrenberg.
A spotter saw three funnel clouds form and disappear.
A small tornado touched down briefly in the Golden Valley just south of state highway 68.  There was no apparent damage.
Downed power lines at 75th Avenue and Camelback Road. Wind gust estimated from damage.
A thunderstorm produced wind gusts to 60 mph reducing visibility to near zero and downing power lines in parts of Kingman.
The Kingman Sheriff's Office reported hailstones up to one inch in diameter.
Street flooding was reported at the intersection of 51st Ave. and Beardsly Rd. A nearby location reported an inch of rain in 30 minutes.
Strong thunderstorm winds uprooted several large trees, destroyed a few sheds, blew down block fences, and flattened a wooden nursery building at the foothills east of Yuma.
Very heavy thunderstorm rains caused street flooding at the Yuma foothills.
A spotter in East Mesa estimated wind gusts to 60 mph with several downed trees.
A small plane ripped away from tie downs at the Scottsdale Airport, damaging it and another plane.
Strong thunderstorm winds knocked down 22 power poles. Wind gust was estimated from damage.
Major street flooding reported at the intersection of Recker Rd and McKellips Rd in East Mesa.

The public and a National Weather Service employee reported a funnel cloud over Ahwatukee. Visual sighting of the dissipating funnel was made from the National Weather Service Forecast Office in Phoenix.
Phoenix Sheriff's Office reported large hail which damaged several cars at Apache Lake. The hail size is an estimate.
Several small trees were blown down and one 25 foot-tall tree was blown over.

Very heavy thundertorm rains caused flash flooding at a campground area along Roosevelt Lake. A three-foot wave of water rushed down the northern slope of Four Peaks, engulfing several cars, trailers, and a motor home, some of which washed into Roosevelt Lake near Bachelor Cove.
Hail reported by a weather spotter near the intersection of 32nd St. and Pecos Rd.
A strong winter storm produced 11 inches of snow at Hannagan Meadows in Greenlee county.
A pilot reported a brief occurrence of a funnel cloud.
Lightning-induced fire caused extensive damage to a home in the 6100 block of East Inglewood Street.
Lightning struck a home in the 1300 block of South Nassau which started a blaze in the attic and caused extensive structural damage and damage to the home's contents.
A horse corral was blown down due to strong thunderstorm winds. Wind gust is estimated from damage.
Several downed power poles.
Very heavy rain fell with thunderstorms which produced flooding of some roads. The heavy rain caused rocks to wash onto Highway 88 in Apache Junction.
Dime-sized hail was reported at Roosevelt Dam.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN APACHE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Between 9:00 AM and 3:00 PM MST, strong gradient winds blew across southern Apache County near St. Johns, with gusts frequently exceeding 70 mph. The peak gust of 87 mph/76 kts occurred at 10:45 AM MST and was recorded at the St. Johns airport.
Hail reported by a National Weather Service employee.
Very heavy thunderstorm rains caused street flooding with water over the curbs.
Very heavy thunderstorm rain caused street flooding. One report of 0.70 inches of rainfall in 20 minutes.
A spotter reported gusts to 60 mph near the intersection of Loop 101 and Bell Rd.
Hail was reported by a spotter near the intersection of Loop 101 and Bell Rd.
Quarter-sized hail reported by a spotter at the intersection of 75th Ave. and Loop 101.
Numerous large trees downed at the intersection of 75th Ave. and Loop 101.
Thunderstorms 10 to 20 miles west of Kingman, Az, produced heavy rains which caused flash flooding in the Golden Valley.  Some roads were made impassable by the flooding.
A tornado touched down near U.S. Highway 93 five miles west of Kingman.  No damage was reported.
Kingman Air Rescue reported golfball-size hailstones covering the ground.

The largest tornado observed during the event touched down near Interstate 40 about 18 miles southwest of Kingman.  The funnel was estimated to be 1/4 mile wide and traveled on an eastward track for about 20 minutes.
One inch hail reported 2 miles south of Catalina
Very heavy rainfall totaling more than two inches caused street flooding in parts of Casa Grande.
A few downed power poles at 32nd St. and Stanford Dr. Wind gust estimated from damage.
Several power poles blown down. Wind gust estimated from damage.
Roads and washes flooded in Fort Huachuca.
Washes flooded and impassable. Tree limbs broken due to high wind in Ajo.  Telephone lines down due to high wind in the town of Why. Several people stranded due to roads cut by running water.
Street flooding in Fort Huachuca.
Tornado confirmed near Ina and Thornydale Roads by Pima County Sheriff, Fire Department, and NWS personnel. Tornado was on the ground for just a few minutes in open country causing no damage.
52 kt wind gust reported near Mission and Los Realis roads.
A strong winter storm produced wind gusts to 58 kt near Safford and 68 kt on Mt Lemmon near Tucson. Widespread blowing dust reduced visibilities over much of the area.
A strong winter storm produced wind gusts to 58 kt near Safford and 68 kt on Mt Lemmon near Tucson. Widespread blowing dust reduced visibilities over much of the area.
A strong winter storm produced 20 inches of snow on Mt Lemmon in Pima county. 13 inches of snow fell at Hannagan Meadow and 14 inches fell at Blue, both in northern Greenlee county.
The National Park Service reported hail up to one inch in diameter near Cottonwood Cove along the Colorado River.
A strong winter storm produced 20 inches of snow on Mt Lemmon in Pima county. 13 inches of snow fell at Hannagan Meadow and 14 inches fell at Blue, both in northern Greenlee county.
Heavy rain caused road closures in the Tucson Metro area.
Thunderstorm winds blew down a couple of power poles at the intersection of Baseline and Recker. Wind gust estimated from damage.

A Gila County Deputy Sheriff witnessed a small tornado which remained on the ground for about 5 to 10 seconds before lifting back off the ground. The tornado moved across the ground about 100 feet. It went through the Alhambra Mobile Home Park and struck a mobile home, lifted it a couple feet off the ground, and then dropped it. The thunderstorm which produced the tornado caused straight-line wind damage, including damage to trees, power lines, and shingled roofs. Damage was confirmed by a NWS survey team.

A wildfire, named the "Slate Fire", was human-caused but wind-driven.  The fire grew to over 200 acres in less than two hours.  No structures were damaged, but a total of 275 acres of timber were destroyed.  The fire was controlled on May 1.
A disturbance moved over Northern Arizona causing windy conditions across much of the state.  Wind speeds reached high wind criteria briefly at St. Johns.
Thunderstorm wind gusts ESTIMATED at 100 mph by a trained Skywarn weather spotter snapped off the top half of a large amateur radio antenna tower. Also, a 400 pound sun collector was moved 5 feet.
U.S.D.A. Forest Service reported sighting 7 funnel clouds near the Chevlon Ranger Station.  None of the funnels touched down and there was no damage reported.
Rapid flooding of the Williamson Valley and Chino Loop roads in Prescott was caused by 2.5 inches of rain in one hour.  No damage or injuries.
1.00 inch diameter hail fell on the Kachina Village area. No damage or injuries reported.

Major winter storm brought heavy snow, strong winds and wind chills below zero.  Blowing and drifting snow was reported on most major highways throughout northern Arizona.  In Zone 4 the North Rim of the Grand Canyon reported 8 inches of new snow.  In Zone 9 Kayenta reported 12 inches, Navajo National Monument reported 10 inches and there were unconfirmed reports of accumulations up to 18 inches.  In Zone 15 Flagstaff received 7 inches and Hart Prairie at 9000 Feet received 9 inches.  In Zone 16 Blue Ridge reported 8 inches of new snow and Heber received 10 inches.  Finally in Zone 18 Calcord Mountain in extreme NE Gila County received 10 inches of new snow.

Several downed power poles. Eight people sustained minor injuries after the strong winds damaged numerous mobile homes in Dateland. Sun Country Acres mobile home park, located two miles north of Interstate 8 on Avenue64E, reported that every mobile home in the park was damaged in some way, many having broken windows. Most of the injuries were to the head and back and cuts from broken glass.
Strong winds knocked down some fences, trees, and power lines. Wind gust was estimated from damage.
Strong winds knocked down some fences, trees, and power lines. Wind gust was estimated from damage.
3/4 inch hail reported near the intersection of Prudence and Golf Links roads.
Flash flooding of streets and washes in the Tucson metro area.  One woman rescued from the Canada Del Oro wash near La Cholla road.
3/4 inch hail reported near St Mary's and Silverbell roads.
65 to 70 mph wind gusts ripped a roof off a home, caused tree damage and power outages in Tucson.
52 kt wind gust blew a one ton truck 100-150 ft.
Flash flooding of underpasses along I-10 near Prince road and Speedway.
Roof blown off a church and a brick fence blown over on Craycroft road.
Flash flooding reported in Tucson at the intersection of Fifth and Wilmot.
Flash flooding with sheet flooding across a flat area with no washes or gulches. 3 inches of rain in one hour with 3.75 inches storm total.

Between 0500 and 1000 am very heavy thunderstorm rainfall produced widespread flash flooding over the Tucson Metro area.  35-40 rescues were made of people stranded in flooded washes, and 250 storm related emergencies were reported to city and county Fire Departments.  Numerous roads were closed, while some roads and water lines were washed out. One home near River Rd and Dodge Blvd was flooded with 6 inches of water and 2 inches of mud. As much as 5 inches of rain fell in a 2 1/2 hour period in the foothills of the Santa Catalina Mountains.
Thunderstorm winds estimated in excess of 40 mph blow down telephone poles near Country Club and Benson.  Five structural fires due to lightning were also reported.
Thunderstorm winds blew windows out of a home, and produced roof damage to several homes.
Roads near Organ Pipe Cactus National Monument were impassable due to flooding.

A spotter in Wenden reported thunderstorm winds which blew down five 60-foot pine trees and caused several thousand dollars damage to his work shed. Power poles were blown down in a two mile stretch along Highway 60 between Wenden and Salome.

Straight-line winds caused widespread damage in Quartzsite. The damage included signs blown down, trailers knocked over, automobile garages flattened, a well-driller workshop damaged, 5 Palo Verde trees uprooted, roof damage, evaporative(swamp) coolers blown off, and a McDonald's Restaurant sign was shattered.

Wind gust estimated by a National Weather Service employee in northern Glendale between Pinnacle Peak Road and Happy Valley Road. The thunderstorm produced one inch diameter hail and the wind caused damage to his house, including roof damage and his chimney being blown into his swimming pool.
Several trees were blown down, and some power poles down. There were also reports of destroyed awnings and storage sheds.
Power lines down.

Substantial damage caused by straight-line winds around 63rd Avenue and Bell Road, including wood and block fences blown down, several downed trees and power poles, and many homes with extensive roof damage. Marble-sized hail and 1.55 inches of rainfall storm total also reported with the storm.
Wind gust was measured by an anemometer at the Deer Valley Tower. This set an all-time record high wind speed (115 mph) in Arizona.
Thunderstorm wind gust to 50 kts/58 mph reported at the Sedona Airport.
Long lived mesocyclone produced several funnel clouds and 0.75 inch hail.
Lightning struck and set fire to a car.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN APACHE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong snow storm produced heavy snow in the higher elevations of central Arizona. In the White Mountains the town of Greer received 12 inches of snow while Pinetop received 7 inches. Along the Mogollon Rim in Flagstaff 8 inches of snow accumulated with 9 inches accumulating at Walnut Canyon National Monument 10 miles east of Flagstaff.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong snow storm produced heavy snow in the higher elevations of central Arizona. In the White Mountains the town of Greer received 12 inches of snow while Pinetop received 7 inches. Along the Mogollon Rim in Flagstaff 8 inches of snow accumulated with 9 inches accumulating at Walnut Canyon National Monument 10 miles east of Flagstaff.
Gradient wind gusts exceeding 60 mph. Max gusts recorded at the St. John's airport: 65 kts/75 mph.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gradient wind gusts exceeding 60 mph/ 52 kts reported by a weather spotter. Gusts estimated over 65 mph, but wind equipment could only measure 60 mph wind speeds.
Thunderstorms with rain rates of 1 inch in 30 minutes produced 5 feet of water in Alamo and Pantano washes.  These washes are normally dry. Numerous power outages and flooded streets in Tucson.
Thunderstorms with heavy rain produced flooding east of Benson and St David in Lonesome Valley.  Benson Airport Rd and Miller Lane were closed due to flooding.
Local Tucson media reported two houses struck by lightning, one house started on fire by lightning.
Flash flooding reported near Kinney Road, 1 mile north of Ajo Road.
Strong thunderstorm winds with visibilities reduced by dust toppled a double wide mobile home in transit on I-10 near Picacho.

A strong winter storm produced snow accumulations of 8 to 15 inches along the Mogollon Rim of north-central Arizona.  Flagstaff received 11 inches of snow.  The highest accumulation of 15 inches was recorded at Parks, 35 miles west of Flagstaff.  Wind speed ranged from 15 to 35 mph producing wind chills to 20 below zero.  The hazardous driving conditions caused over one hundred vehicle accidents within a 30 mile radius of Flagstaff, Arizona.  Only minor injuries were reported.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN APACHE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Brief high wind event.  One measured gust to 60 mph reported by a weather spotter.
Six inches of snow accumulated at the South Rim of the Grand Canyon.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: In Flagstaff, AZ and along the Mogollon Rim 6.0 to 7.2 inches of snow accumulated.  The heaviest accumulation, 7.2 inches, was recorded in Flagstaff.
Widespread high wind event.  Peak wind reported at Winslow Airport of 53 kt/61 mph.

Widespread high wind event. One mobile home roof torn off by winds in St. Johns with roof damage to many houses and mobile homes.  In Springerville a barn roof was torn off and one mobile home was pushed over on its' side. In both cities several metal sheds tipped over and mobile homes were moved off foundations.  Two minor auto accidents caused by crosswinds.  In St. Johns, one 78 kt/90 mph gust was reported at 10:25 AM, and another 78 kt/90 mph gust reported at 1:50 PM. In Winslow on gust to 53 kt/61 mph was reported at 900 AM.  In Springerville a peak gust of 86 kt/99 mph was reported at 10:35 AM.
Tucson Mountain Park and Snyder Hill Road closed due to flash flooding.
Two dozen eucalyptus trees were snapped in half and several power poles were knocked down.
Strong thunderstorms ripped through the Gila Bend area which toppled 23 power poles.

Casa Grande firefighters treated an 86-year-old man who had fallen after a storage shed blew up against his mobile home and knocked him off balance in the 500 block of Avenue A. The shed had blown into nearby power and cable television lines. A large piece of the shed also knocked down a 30-40-foot section of a 6-foot-tall wood fence. Firefighters also treated a 29-year-old man who was injured in the 600 Block of North Cameron Avenue after strong winds "threw" him against a building. Wind gust was estimated from damage.
Several large trees were blown over, power poles were blown down, mobile homes were overturned, and buildings were ripped apart. A window was blown out of a mobile home injuring a young woman.
Shingles blown off many homes, roof damage, trees and fences down at 52nd Avenue and Union Hills Road.
Roofs were blown off two buildings and trees were downed.
Several swift water rescues were made of people stranded in the rising waters in streets and washes.  Flooding reported at the intersection of Pantano and Irvington.
Wind related tree damage was reported in Starr Pass. Damage to trees and business signs occurred near Sixth Ave and Ajo Rd.

Rattlesnake Wash filled rapidly with water from heavy thunderstorm rains, flooding low-water crossings on Perkinsville Road in extreme southwest Coconino County. A motorcyclist suffered minor injuries when he attempted to cross the wash away from one of the established crossings. He and his cylce were carried downstream. His motorcycle was extensively damaged.
A strong thunderstorm created street flooding in south and southeast Tucson. Cars were stranded with 6 inches to two feet of water over roadways.
A hailstorm, lasting approximately 30 minutes, dropped up to one inch diameter hail and left the ground covered one to two inches deep.
Heavy thunderstorm rain flooded Lake Mary Road with over 3 inches of water covering the road.
Funnel cloud spotted by the public.
Roof damage was reported by a spotter.
Wind gust reported by a spotter at 27th Avenue and McDowell Road.

Thunderstorms produced heavy rain as they moved through Scottsdale.Indian Bend wash, which is normally dry, flooded quickly and forcedtwo roads to close. There was a report of street flooding at Scottsdale Road and McDowell Road. A spotter in South Scottsdale reported a storm total of .80 inches of rainfall. Wind gusts to 45 mph accompanied the storms.
Thunderstorm winds blew roof tiles and a metal awning off a house, and a shed roof was blown 120 yards.
A funnel cloud was spotted by a pilot.

CZ_NAME: NORTHEAST PLATEAUS AND MESAS FROM HIGHWAY 264 NORTH
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Major winter storm brought heavy snow, strong winds and wind chills below zero.  Blowing and drifting snow was reported on most major highways throughout northern Arizona.  In Zone 4 the North Rim of the Grand Canyon reported 8 inches of new snow.  In Zone 9 Kayenta reported 12 inches, Navajo National Monument reported 10 inches and there were unconfirmed reports of accumulations up to 18 inches.  In Zone 15 Flagstaff received 7 inches and Hart Prairie at 9000 Feet received 9 inches.  In Zone 16 Blue Ridge reported 8 inches of new snow and Heber received 10 inches.  Finally in Zone 18 Calcord Mountain in extreme NE Gila County received 10 inches of new snow.
Thunderstorms with very heavy rainfall 5-10 s of Sierra Vista washed out numerous roads and water lines.  Boulders as large as cars were deposited on Hereford Road.
Strong thunderstorm winds and local street flooding.
Thunderstorm flash flooding washed out Hereford Road.

Major winter storm brought heavy snow, strong winds and wind chills below zero.  Blowing and drifting snow was reported on most major highways throughout northern Arizona.  In Zone 4 the North Rim of the Grand Canyon reported 8 inches of new snow.  In Zone 9 Kayenta reported 12 inches, Navajo National Monument reported 10 inches and there were unconfirmed reports of accumulations up to 18 inches.  In Zone 15 Flagstaff received 7 inches and Hart Prairie at 9000 Feet received 9 inches.  In Zone 16 Blue Ridge reported 8 inches of new snow and Heber received 10 inches.  Finally in Zone 18 Calcord Mountain in extreme NE Gila County received 10 inches of new snow.
A section of a road was washed out by rushing flood waters.

Blowing dust between Bowie and San Simon caused the closure of Interstate 10 between 1pm and 2pm when the visibility was reduced to near zero. Blowing dust was widespread across much of Cochise county due to west winds in the 30 to 40 mph range with occasional gusts to 50 mph.

Gusty thunderstorm winds caused damage and a few injuries at a Fry's Food Store at the intersection of Power Road and Baseline Road in East Mesa. The winds lifted a 2000 pound tent and slammed it against a truck. The glass-covered artwork beneath the tent shattered and caused minor cuts to two people. A teenage boy was thrown into a grocery cart corral and treated at a local hospital for back injuries. Several cars in the parking lot sustained dents from flying debris.
Strong thunderstorm winds caused $350,000 damage to gliders and structures at Avra Valley glider airport.
A National Weather Service employee reported strong winds, 1/2 inch diameter hail, and heavy rain which caused minor street flooding.
A spotter reported strong thunderstorm winds.
A few power lines were downed by thunderstorm winds at the intersection of Granite Reef Rd and McDonald Dr. Wind gust was estimated.
Thunderstorm related winds, estimated by law enforcement officers to be between 50 to 60 mph, reduced visibility to near zero in Kayenta and Tuba City.  No damage was reported.
Thunderstorm gust reported by a spotter at the intersection of 75th Ave. and Loop 101.
Large hail reported by a National Weather Service employee.
Very strong thunderstorm winds knocked down some block walls at the intersection of 51st Ave. and Beardsly Rd. Wind gust was estimated.
Brief funnel cloud sighted by a spotter just north of the intersection of 43rd Ave. and Bell Rd.
Heavy rain flooded Higley Road in East Mesa. A spotter there recorded 1.45 inches of rainfall in 30 minutes.
Funnel cloud was spotted by a National Weather Service employee.
Power lines blown down near the intersection of Stapley Rd and Mesa Dr. Wind gust was estimated.
Several power lines blown down from 67th Ave. to 75th Ave. just south of Indian School Rd. Wind gust was estimated.
Several power lines reported blown down. Wind gust was estimated.

Glendale was one of the hardest hit areas after very powerful thunderstorms ripped through the area. The storm collapsed a the roof of an apartment complex and blew metal dumpsters weighing up to two tons into roadways. Sahuaro Ranch School, 10401 N. 63rd Ave., lost part of its roof. An Albertsons grocery store at 59th Avenue and Beardsley Road was battered by the storm, leaving three people hurt. The storm did extensive damage to the roof of Arrowhead Community Hospital, 18701 N. 67th Avenue. At the Northwest Garden Apartments, 9350 N. 67th Avenue, residents were evacuated after the storm collapsed the roof over eight units. Shingles and twisted sheets of park awnings were scattered across the complex. At the Adobe Mountain Juvenile Correctional Facility, one staff member was slightly injured when windows blew out in one of the units. Fences around the perimeters of the facility were damaged by falling trees. About 40 vehicles were damaged, two of them hit by a dumpster blown through the parking lot.

Two juvenile detention centers, the Adobe Mountain Secure Facility and the Black Canyon Secure Facility, both in north Phoenix, sustained an estimated $250,000 combined damage when strong winds damaged a perimeter fence, blew out a plastic glass window and damaged severaly roofs at the complex. Two support pillars under construction at the Interstate 17 and Loop 101 interchange in north Phoenix buckled from the high winds, with an estimated $250,000 damage.
Some power-line wires and large tree limbs were downed. Wind gust was estimated from damage.

Downed power lines, broken and uprooted trees, roof shingles and mangled metal awnings were scattered in yards and streets throughout the city. A block fence blew down at Peoria Westbrook Village. High winds sent a tree sailing through the front of Pioneer Elementary School, 6315 W. Port au Prince Lane. The winds twisted a street sign in the opposite direction at the corner of 83rd Avenue and Washington Street.
Two parked and empty railroad car-carriers were blown over onto their sides. Wind gust was estimated.

Every town in the western half of the Phoenix Metropolitan Area reported some damage. The hardest hit areas were in northwest Phoenix, Glendale, and Peoria. Other towns that sustained damage were Sun City, Surprise, El Mirage, Tolleson, Avondale, Goodyear, and Buckeye. Approximately 400 power poles were knocked down throughout these towns, 100 owned by SRP and 300 owned by APS. There were from 70,000 to 75,000 homeowner claims for about $100 million in damage.
Heavy thunderstorm rain caused small stream flooding reported across Interstate 40 near Meteor City, which is east of Flagstaff.
Heavy thunderstorm rain with small hail flooded several dirt county roads causing mud and water to wash across main roads in town.
Heavy rains from thunderstorms flooded fields. Spotter reports indicated in excess of 2 inches storm total with some areas receiving 1 inch in 15 minutes.
Flash flooding caused some road closures near Bowie.
1.6 inches of rain in 1.5 hours caused 4 inches to 1 foot of water over low lying areas between Bisbee and Palominas. Many roads in the area impassable due to water over the roadway.

Thunderstorm rains caused minor street flooding in Tucson. Spotter near Vail reported just over 1 inch of rain in 25 minutes. Seven cars were stalled in high water on Grant road on the east side of Tucson.

High winds and visibilities to near zero at times from blowing dust triggered a two-accident, nine car pileup at Interstate 10 and Chandler Boulevard. Eleven people suffered minor injuries. An unseasonably strong storm system and associated cold front was responsible for the high winds.
Small stream flooding. The Aqua Fria River flooded over River Bend Road at Black Canyon City. The flooding was caused by thunderstorm rain and closed River Bend Road for 40 minutes.
Thunderstorm rain caused street flooding in the town of Strawberry. Several roads were flooded with ponding water.
Heavy rain from a thunderstorm flooded roadway dips on Arivaca Road.
1.5 inches rain in 20 minutes accompanied the strong winds.
3/4 inch hail was reported 8 miles north of Davis Montham Air Force base.

A thunderstorm moving through a remote area knocked down 25 power poles cutting power to Amado. Power in Amado was out fo 36 hours following the wind damage. A mobile home was also overturned and demolished near Sahuarita.
Strong thunderstorm winds caused tree damage.
Strong thunderstorm winds blow a 2 inch limb off a cottonwood tree in Elgin.
Hail reported at the intersection of Sabino and Tanque Verde Rds, and near Fifth and Columbus.
Heavy thunderstorm rains caused ditches along Highway 87 to flood across the highway near Clint's Well. Water covered highway 87 for an hour.
Golfball sized hail with heavy rain produced near zero visibility and minor rock slides along highway 89 south of Prescott.
Strong winds blew a semi-trailer off highway 260 near Kayenta. Damage unknown.
Heavy thunderstorm rain produced street flooding in White River, southeast of Show Low. Also, near White River several small streams and washes flooded.
Thunderstorm downburst winds caused roof damage at the Dateland Farm Plaza. Shingles and tar paper were blown off the roof.
Two inch diameter tree limbs snapped from several trees and shingles were blown off homes.

Major winter storm brought heavy snow, strong winds and wind chills below zero.  Blowing and drifting snow was reported on most major highways throughout northern Arizona.  In Zone 4 the North Rim of the Grand Canyon reported 8 inches of new snow.  In Zone 9 Kayenta reported 12 inches, Navajo National Monument reported 10 inches and there were unconfirmed reports of accumulations up to 18 inches.  In Zone 15 Flagstaff received 7 inches and Hart Prairie at 9000 Feet received 9 inches.  In Zone 16 Blue Ridge reported 8 inches of new snow and Heber received 10 inches.  Finally in Zone 18 Calcord Mountain in extreme NE Gila County received 10 inches of new snow.

Major winter storm brought heavy snow, strong winds and wind chills below zero.  Blowing and drifting snow was reported on most major highways throughout northern Arizona.  In Zone 4 the North Rim of the Grand Canyon reported 8 inches of new snow.  In Zone 9 Kayenta reported 12 inches, Navajo National Monument reported 10 inches and there were unconfirmed reports of accumulations up to 18 inches.  In Zone 15 Flagstaff received 7 inches and Hart Prairie at 9000 Feet received 9 inches.  In Zone 16 Blue Ridge reported 8 inches of new snow and Heber received 10 inches.  Finally in Zone 18 Calcord Mountain in extreme NE Gila County received 10 inches of new snow.
Several large trees blown down.
A spotter reported a funnel cloud at 63rd Avenue and Bell Road.
Heavy thunderstorm rain produced flooded several streets in east Flagstaff.
Funnel Cloud reported by the public and confirmed by law enforcement officer.

Heavy rain from thunderstorms hit the Tucson area with flash flooding in many washes. The southwest side and northeast side of Tucson were hit hardest. One fireman was injured while checking if a car that had been washed off Harrison Road had anybody in it. The Ajo and Mission Road areas received 2.5 inches of rain in 45 minutes.
Several reports of street flooding around Tucson.
Hwy 86 closed due to flash flooding from Sells to Ajo.
Heavy rain and flash flooding resulted in 5 feet of water in Pantano wash near vail.  This wash is normally dry.
A 52 kt wind gust caused tree damage.
52 kt wind gust reported near I-10 and Wilmot road.
Street flooding reported near 4th Ave and 6th Street.

A strong thunderstorm moved through the Tucson area with wind related damage. A small shed was blown through a fence and destroyed. A roof was blown off a double wide mobile home and carried across neighboring homes. Roofs were blown off and windows blown out of trailers. At Tucson International Airport, 4 airplanes were damaged and the roof was blown off a hanger. One to two feet diameter trees were snapped. A spotter 6SE of Tucson measured a 57 Kt wind. The 82 Kt wind from this storm was recorded at Davis Monthon Air Base.

63 kt wind gust reported near Wilmot and Golf Links road.  Roof blown off a home, structural damaged to an Elementary school, tree damage and power lines down. Visibility was near zero in blowing dust across most of southeast Tucson as the storm moved in from the east.
Strong thunderstorm winds caused roof damage south of Winkelman.
A strong thunderstorm produced .75 diameter inch hail at Ski Valley on Mt Lemmon. The hail accumulated to a depth of 4 inches on the ground.
A strong thunderstorm produced flash flooding and wind damage near Taylor and Ajo Roads. The water was running one foot deep and a Mobile home rolled over.
A quick 6 to 8 inches of snow fell on the Kaibab Plateau. Eight inches total accumulation was reported at Jacob Lake and the North Rim of the Grand Canyon.
Heavy snow fell above 8500 feet MSL.  At 9000 feet 10 inches of wet snow accumulated at Hart Prairie.
Strong wind blew shingles of roofs at Marana Airport.  Three power poles blown down on Trico road.
Woman struck by lightning inside her house while vacuming.

A tornado moved through the east end of Chino Valley. Two mobile homes were moved 7 feet off their foundations and received moderate exterior and interior damage. Several power poles were snapped off at 3 feet above the ground. One wood frame home received extensive roof damage where 95% of the roof surface was removed. Eight other wood frame homes received light to moderate roof damage. One greenhouse was extensively damaged where 18 foot long wood planks were thrown 100 feet and 850 plants were destroyed. Many sheds were completely destroyed.
Thunderstorm gust recorded at the Winslow Airport.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN APACHE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread high wind event. One mobile home roof torn off by winds in St. Johns with roof damage to many houses and mobile homes.  In Springerville a barn roof was torn off and one mobile home was pushed over on its' side. In both cities several metal sheds tipped over and mobile homes were moved off foundations.  Two minor auto accidents caused by crosswinds.  In St. Johns, one 78 kt/90 mph gust was reported at 10:25 AM, and another 78 kt/90 mph gust reported at 1:50 PM. In Winslow on gust to 53 kt/61 mph was reported at 900 AM.  In Springerville a peak gust of 86 kt/99 mph was reported at 10:35 AM.
